story.iWant: to download all slides as PNG images in a ZIP file
story.soThat: I can archive the deck or use slides individually

task.title: Inline JSZip library
task.subtask: Add to script section before application code

task.title: Add progress modal HTML/CSS
task.subtask: Modal overlay with dark semi-transparent background
Centered card with title and progress bar
Progress text showing current slide count

task.title: Implement progress modal functions
task.subtask: updateProgress(current, total) - Update bar and text

task.title: Implement downloadAllSlidesPNG() function
task.subtask: For each slide: load in iframe, capture with html2canvas, add to zip

task.title: Wire up menu item
task.subtask: Connect "Download All Slides" menu item to function

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Given I am viewing slides in presentation mode, When I click "Download All Slides (ZIP)" from the download menu, Then a progress modal appears
The progress bar updates as each slide is processed (e.g., "Processing slide 3 of 14...")
When complete, a ZIP file downloads automatically
The ZIP contains PNG files for each slide numbered sequentially (slide-1.png, slide-2.png, etc.)
The progress modal dismisses after download

doc.title: Technical Specification - Epic 12
doc.section: Implementation Details - Story 3: Batch PNG Export (ZIP)
doc.snippet: Use JSZip to bundle individual slide PNGs into a single downloadable ZIP file. Process slides sequentially (not parallel) to manage memory usage. Show a progress modal to indicate activity and provide feedback during the potentially lengthy batch operation.

doc.title: Technical Specification
doc.snippet: JSZip v3.10.1 (~45KB minified). ZIP file naming: {deckName}-slides.zip. PNG files numbered slide-1.png, slide-2.png, etc.

doc.title: Technical Specification
doc.snippet: function updateProgress(current, total) { const percent = Math.round((current / total) * 100); progressBar.style.width = percent + '%'; progressText.textContent = 'Processing slide ' + current + ' of ' + total + '...'; }

doc.title: Technical Specification
doc.snippet: For large decks (20+ slides): Process slides sequentially (not parallel) to limit memory. Release canvas blobs after adding to ZIP/PDF. Show progress to indicate activity.

file.reason: Placeholder function to replace with full implementation - currently shows alert
Existing slide capture function using html2canvas - must be adapted for batch capture with slide index parameter
Pattern for downloading PNG - use downloadBlob and sanitizeFilename utilities
Utility function for triggering downloads - reuse for ZIP file
Utility for safe filenames - use for ZIP filename
Download menu already has "Download All Slides (ZIP)" button wired to downloadAllSlidesPNG()
Existing CSS patterns for download UI - follow for progress modal styling
Global variables needed for batch processing - slides array, total count, deck name

inline.note: MUST BE ADDED - Download and inline before application code

constraints.constraint: Single file modification - all changes in .slide-builder/config/templates/viewer-template.html
Must work with file:// protocol - no server dependencies
Process slides sequentially, not in parallel, to manage memory
Use existing html2canvas captureSlide pattern but adapt for batch with slide index parameter
Follow existing code style: camelCase, inline onclick handlers, CSS variables for colors
JSZip library must be inlined before application code in script section
Progress modal styling must match existing dark theme (--amp-dark-alt, --amp-lime, --amp-mid-gray)
ZIP filename must be sanitized using existing sanitizeFilename utility
Progress modal must be non-dismissible during export (intentional UX)
After regenerating viewer, test with: node scripts/regenerate-viewer.js claude-code-fundamentals

interface.name: captureSlideForExport
interface.note: New function - adapts captureSlide to accept slide index and return PNG blob directly

interface.name: showProgressModal
interface.note: New function - shows progress modal with title

interface.name: updateProgress
interface.signature: function updateProgress(current: number, total: number): void
interface.note: New function - updates progress bar and text

interface.name: downloadAllSlidesPNG
interface.note: Replace placeholder - full implementation for batch export

interface.name: JSZip
interface.note: JSZip v3.10.1 API for creating ZIP files

tests.standards: No automated testing framework in place. All testing is manual browser-based. Test in Chrome (primary), Safari, and Firefox. Open DevTools console for error monitoring during tests.

locations.location: Manual testing via browser - open index.html directly (file://) and via python3 -m http.server (http://)

ideas.idea: Click "Download All Slides (ZIP)" and verify progress modal appears immediately
Watch progress bar update - should show "Processing slide X of Y..." for each slide
Verify ZIP file downloads automatically when progress reaches 100%
Check downloaded filename matches pattern: {deckName}-slides.zip (e.g., claude-code-fundamentals-slides.zip)
Unzip file and verify contains slide-1.png through slide-N.png, each 1920x1080
Verify progress modal dismisses after download completes
Test with file:// protocol: Open index.html directly from filesystem, not via server
Test with http:// protocol: Serve via python3 -m http.server and verify works there too
Test large deck: Use 14-slide claude-code-fundamentals deck, verify no memory issues
Cross-browser: Test in Chrome, Safari, Firefox
Check console for errors during export process
